name: SourceFire DNS Tunneling Detection - Multiple domains
description: Detects a single source IP address making a large number of DNS queries for different domains. This behavior is a strong indicator of DNS tunneling, a technique used by adversaries to establish a command and control channel or exfiltrate data by encapsulating it within DNS queries.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered: a single source IP address, **{source_address}**, has made a high volume of DNS queries for more than 50 different domains within a short period. This is a strong indicator of a **DNS Tunneling** attack (**MITRE ATT&CK T1090.004**), which is a common technique for **Command and Control** and **Data Exfiltration**. Immediate investigation is required to block the malicious IP address and to secure the affected host.

parameters.name: source_address
parameters.description: The source IP address that is performing the suspicious DNS queries.
